name: grinding-it-out
description: Ray Kroc's autobiography of building McDonald's — a story of relentless persistence, quality obsession, and entrepreneurial grit. Covers: discovering opportunity, building systems, managing franchise relationships, real estate strategy, and scaling a business.
useCase: Learn how a 52-year-old paper cup salesman built the world's largest restaurant chain through obsessive attention to quality, systemization, and grinding it out.
knownRisks: Kroc's methods were aggressive and not always ethical by today's standards — the book describes them frankly.
The autobiography reflects Kroc's personal perspective; other accounts of McDonald's history differ on key events.
The franchise model Kroc pioneered has been criticized for its impact on local communities and labor practices.
